Statement (IV) is incorrect because the synthesis of the $\beta$-chain is controlled by a single gene, HBB, located on chromosome 11, not chromosome 16.

Therefore, Statements (I) and (II) are correct while (III) and (IV) are incorrect.

Answer: Statement (I) and (II) are correct while (III) and (IV) are incorrect

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Study the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement (I): Examples of Mendelian disorders are Turner's syndrome and Down's syndrome.
Statement (II): Mendelian disorders are caused due to alternation or mutation in the gene.
Statement (III): Chromosomal disorders are caused due to the absence or excess of one or more chromosomes
options: Statement II is correct and I and III are incorrect
Statement I, II and III are correct
Statement I is incorrect and II, III are correct
Statement I, II and III are incorrect
questions.solution:
Statement I is incorrect because Turner's syndrome and Down's syndrome are examples of chromosomal disorders, not Mendelian disorders. Examples of Mendelian disorders include haemophilia, cystic fibrosis, sickle-cell anaemia, etc.

Statement II is correct as Mendelian disorders are mainly determined by alteration or mutation in a single gene.

Statement III is correct as chromosomal disorders are caused due to the absence or excess or abnormal arrangement of one or more chromosomes.

Therefore, Statement I is incorrect and Statements II and III are correct.

Answer: Statement I is incorrect and II, III are correct

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: A sickle-cell carrier man marries a woman who is carrier for sickle-cell anaemia. What are the chances of having carrier children?
options: All the children will be carriers.
None of them will be carriers.
questions.solution:
Sickle-cell anaemia is an autosomal recessive disorder.

Let the normal allele be $Hb^A$ and the sickle-cell allele be $Hb^S$.

The genotype of the carrier man is $Hb^A Hb^S$ and the carrier woman is $Hb^A Hb^S$.

The cross between them is $Hb^A Hb^S \times Hb^A Hb^S$.

The possible genotypes of the offspring are:
$25\%$ $Hb^A Hb^A$ (Normal)
$50\%$ $Hb^A Hb^S$ (Carrier)
$25\%$ $Hb^S Hb^S$ (Affected)

The chances of having carrier children is $50\%$.

Answer: $50\%$ of them will be carriers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In a population with two alleles B and b with frequency $0.6$ and $0.4$ in Hardy-Weinberg equillibrium. How many heterozygous individuals in a sample of $250$ can be expected?
questions.solution:
According to the Hardy-Weinberg principle, the frequency of alleles in a population is represented by $p$ and $q$.

Given the frequency of allele B is $p = 0.6$ and the frequency of allele b is $q = 0.4$.

The frequency of heterozygous individuals is given by $2pq$.

$2pq = 2 \times 0.6 \times 0.4 = 0.48$

The expected number of heterozygous individuals in a sample of $250$ is $2pq \times 250$.

$0.48 \times 250 = 120$

Answer: $120$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: A pea plant with genotype RrYy is crossed with another pea plant with genotype rryy. What will be the number of phenotypes obtained by this cross?
questions.solution:
The given cross is between a dihybrid plant (RrYy) and a homozygous recessive plant (rryy).

This is a dihybrid test cross.

The plant with genotype RrYy produces four types of gametes: RY, Ry, rY, and ry.

The plant with genotype rryy produces only one type of gamete: ry.

The offspring produced from this cross will have the following genotypes and phenotypes:

RrYy : Round Yellow
Rryy : Round Green
rrYy : Wrinkled Yellow
rryy : Wrinkled Green

Thus, four different phenotypes are obtained in a ratio of $1:1:1:1$.

Answer: $4$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following is a symptom of Klinefelter's Syndrome?
questions.solution:
Klinefelter's syndrome is a genetic disorder caused by the presence of an additional copy of the X chromosome, resulting in a karyotype of 47, XXY.

Individuals with this syndrome have overall masculine development, but feminine characteristics are also expressed. One of the prominent symptoms is the development of breasts, which is known as Gynaecomastia.

Webbed neck, broad shield-shaped chest, and lower posterior hair line are symptoms associated with Turner's syndrome (45, XO).

Answer: Gynaecomastia

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: A normal woman whose father was colourblind is married to a normal man, (assuming equal distribution of female gametes), then _________.
options: $50\%$ of their sons will be colourblind.
All the sons will be colourblind.
All the sons will be normal.
$75\%$ of the sons will be colourblind.
questions.solution:
Colour blindness is an X-linked recessive trait.

Let $X^C$ be the normal allele and $X^c$ be the allele for colour blindness.

The woman's father was colourblind ($X^c Y$), so he passed the $X^c$ chromosome to her. Since she is normal, her genotype is $X^C X^c$ (carrier).

The normal man has the genotype $X^C Y$.

A cross between the carrier woman ($X^C X^c$) and the normal man ($X^C Y$) produces the following offspring:

Daughters: $X^C X^C$ (normal) and $X^C X^c$ (normal carrier).

Sons: $X^C Y$ (normal) and $X^c Y$ (colourblind).

Since the sons receive their X chromosome from the mother, who produces $X^C$ and $X^c$ gametes in equal proportions, $50\%$ of the sons will be normal and $50\%$ will be colourblind.

Answer: $50\%$ of their sons will be colourblind.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In the $F_2$ generation, the genotypic and phenotypic ratios are identical in ____.
questions.solution:
In a Mendelian monohybrid cross, the phenotypic ratio in the $F_2$ generation is $3:1$, whereas the genotypic ratio is $1:2:1$.

In the case of co-dominance (and incomplete dominance), the heterozygous condition produces a distinct phenotype where both alleles are expressed equally. When two heterozygotes are crossed, the $F_2$ generation yields a genotypic ratio of $1:2:1$ (homozygous dominant : heterozygous : homozygous recessive) and a phenotypic ratio of $1:2:1$.

Thus, the genotypic and phenotypic ratios are identical in co-dominance.

Answer: Co-dominance

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In X and Y chromosomes, crossing over occurs only between ____.
options: homologous regions of both X and Y
non-homologus regions of both X and Y
non-homologous region of X and homologous region of Y
homologous region of X and non-homologous region of Y
questions.solution:
The X and Y chromosomes are structurally different but share short regions of homology known as pseudoautosomal regions (PARs) at their tips.

During meiosis in males, the X and Y chromosomes pair up and undergo crossing over (recombination) exclusively within these homologous regions.

The non-homologous regions of the X and Y chromosomes do not undergo crossing over, which prevents the exchange of sex-determining genes (like the SRY gene on the Y chromosome).

Therefore, crossing over occurs only between the homologous regions of both X and Y chromosomes.

Answer: homologous regions of both X and Y

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
How many of the following statements are correct about Mendelian alleles?
(A). The dominant allele expresses itself in the presence of an alternative allele.
(B). The recessive allele does not express in the presence of the dominant allele.
(C). The $F_1$ hybrid produced in a cross between homozygous dominant and homozygous recessive is heterozygous.
(D). A homozygous individual for a trait breeds true and produces two different types of gametes.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Statement (A) is correct. A dominant allele is defined as the one that expresses itself even in the presence of an alternative (recessive) allele in a heterozygous condition.

Statement (B) is correct. A recessive allele is masked and does not express its phenotypic effect in the presence of a dominant allele.

Statement (C) is correct. A cross between a homozygous dominant (e.g., $TT$) and a homozygous recessive (e.g., $tt$) yields an $F_1$ generation that is entirely heterozygous ($Tt$).

Statement (D) is incorrect. A homozygous individual for a trait breeds true but produces only one type of gamete, not two different types.

Therefore, only statements (A), (B), and (C) are correct.

Answer: (A), (B) and (C) only.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following could NOT be the genotypes of the parents of a child with the blood group "O" $\text{Rh}^{+ve}$?
questions.solution:
Blood group O requires the genotype $I^0 I^0$.

For a child to have blood group O, both parents must contribute an $I^0$ allele.

Parents with the genotype $I^A I^B$ do not possess the $I^0$ allele. Therefore, they can only produce offspring with blood groups A, B, or AB (genotypes $I^A I^A$, $I^B I^B$, or $I^A I^B$).

Thus, parents with genotypes $I^A I^B\ \text{Rh}^{+ve}$ and $I^A I^B\ \text{Rh}^{+ve}$ cannot have a child with blood group O.

Answer: $I^A I^B\ \text{Rh}^{+ve}$ and $I^A I^B\ \text{Rh}^{+ve}$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following crosses will have all the offsprings with recessive phenotype in the F$_2$ generation?
questions.solution:
For all offspring in the F$_2$ generation to have a recessive phenotype, the initial parents must be homozygous recessive for all the traits.

In the cross rryy $\times$ rryy, both parents are homozygous recessive. They will produce only one type of gamete (ry).

The F$_1$ generation will all have the genotype rryy, which expresses the recessive phenotype.

When the F$_1$ individuals are crossed (rryy $\times$ rryy) to produce the F$_2$ generation, all the resulting offspring will again have the genotype rryy.

Therefore, all offspring in the F$_2$ generation will exhibit the recessive phenotype.

Answer: rryy x rryy

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In sickle cell anemia, the carrier (heterozygous) individuals can be identified by the microscopic examination of ____ cells.
questions.solution:
Sickle cell anemia is an autosomal recessive genetic disorder that affects the shape of red blood cells. Heterozygous individuals (carriers) possess one normal allele and one mutated allele ($Hb^A Hb^S$). Under conditions of low oxygen tension, the red blood cells of these carriers undergo sickling, which can be easily observed through the microscopic examination of blood cells.

Answer: blood

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following disorder shows symptoms of excess of phenylalanine in urine of humans?
questions.solution:
Phenylketonuria is an inborn error of metabolism inherited as an autosomal recessive trait.

The affected individual lacks an enzyme called phenylalanine hydroxylase that converts the amino acid phenylalanine into tyrosine.

As a result, phenylalanine accumulates and gets converted into phenylpyruvic acid and other derivatives.

Accumulation of these in the brain results in mental retardation. These are also excreted through urine because of their poor absorption by the kidney.

Answer: Phenylketonuria

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Match List I with List II
List I (Genetic disorders)List II (Type)
(A). Thalassemia(I). X-monosomy
(B). Turner's syndrome(II). Extra X-chromosome
(C). Klinefelter's syndrome(III). 21$^{st}$ Trisomy
(D). Down's syndrome(IV). Mendelian disorder
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Thalassemia is an autosomal recessive blood disorder, which is classified as a Mendelian disorder. Thus, (A) matches with (IV).

Turner's syndrome is caused by the absence of one of the X chromosomes, resulting in a karyotype of $45$, X0, which is an X-monosomy. Thus, (B) matches with (I).

Klinefelter's syndrome is caused by the presence of an additional copy of the X-chromosome, resulting in a karyotype of $47$, XXY. Thus, (C) matches with (II).

Down's syndrome is caused by the presence of an additional copy of chromosome number $21$, known as $21^{st}$ Trisomy. Thus, (D) matches with (III).

The correct matching is (A) - (IV), (B) - (I), (C) - (II), (D) - (III).

Answer: (A) - (IV), (B) - (I), (C) - (II), (D) - (III)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Identify the INCORRECT statement about red-green color blindness in human.
options: It is a disorder where a person fails to distinguish between red and green colours.
It is due to the absence of cones in the retina of the eye.
It is inherited from a colorblind father to his son.
A dominant X linked gene is necessary for the formation of cones.
questions.solution:
Red-green colour blindness is a sex-linked recessive disorder caused by a defect in the genes located on the X chromosome.

Males have an XY sex chromosome pair, while females have XX. A father passes his Y chromosome to his son and his X chromosome to his daughter.

Since the gene for red-green colour blindness is present on the X chromosome, a colourblind father cannot pass this defective gene to his son. Instead, criss-cross inheritance is observed where the trait is passed from a carrier mother to her son.

Thus, the statement that it is inherited from a colourblind father to his son is incorrect.

Answer: It is inherited from a colorblind father to his son.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Morgan carried out several dihybrid crosses in Drosophila. Which one of the following crosses helped him find the sex-linked inheritance?
options: Yellow-bodied, white-eyed female with the wild type with brown-bodied, red-eyed male.
Yellow-bodied, red-eyed female with yellow-bodied, white-eyed male.
Brown-bodied, white-eyed male with yellow-bodied white-eyed female.
Yellow-bodied, red-eyed male with white-bodied, white-eyed female.
questions.solution:
Morgan carried out several dihybrid crosses in Drosophila to study genes that were sex-linked. The crosses were similar to the dihybrid crosses carried out by Mendel in peas.

Morgan hybridized yellow-bodied, white-eyed females to brown-bodied, red-eyed males (wild type) and intercrossed their F1 progeny. He observed that the two genes did not segregate independently of each other and the F2 ratio deviated very significantly from the expected $9:3:3:1$ ratio. This cross helped him establish the concept of sex-linked inheritance and linkage.

Answer: Yellow-bodied, white-eyed female with the wild type with brown-bodied, red-eyed male.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Match List I with List II
List I (Gene interaction)List II (Examples)
(A). Holandric genes(I). Multiple effects of a single gene
(B). Pleiotropy(II). Hypertrichosis
(C). Multiple alleles(III). ABO blood types
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Holandric genes are genes located on the Y chromosome and are transmitted directly from father to son. Hypertrichosis (excessive hair on the ear pinna) is a classic example of a holandric trait. Thus, (A) matches with (II).

Pleiotropy is the phenomenon where a single gene exhibits multiple phenotypic expressions or effects. Thus, (B) matches with (I).

Multiple alleles refer to the existence of more than two alternative forms of a gene in a population. The ABO blood group system in humans is a well-known example, governed by three alleles ($I^A$, $I^B$, and $i$). Thus, (C) matches with (III).

The correct matching is (A) - (II), (B) - (I), (C) - (III).

Answer: (A) - (II), (B) - (I), (C) - (III)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In a cross between a pure tall pea plant with yellow pod and a pure short plant with green pod, how many double recessive plants would you expect in $F_2$ generation?
questions.solution:
In pea plants, tallness ($T$) is dominant over shortness ($t$), and green pod colour ($G$) is dominant over yellow pod colour ($g$).

The cross is between a pure tall plant with yellow pods ($TTgg$) and a pure short plant with green pods ($ttGG$).

The $F_1$ generation will have the genotype $TtGg$, which corresponds to tall plants with green pods.

Upon selfing the $F_1$ generation ($TtGg \times TtGg$), the $F_2$ generation is produced.

The phenotypic ratio of the $F_2$ generation in a dihybrid cross is $9:3:3:1$, which represents:
$9$ Tall, Green pods
$3$ Tall, Yellow pods
$3$ Short, Green pods
$1$ Short, Yellow pod

The double recessive phenotype is the short plant with yellow pods ($ttgg$).

Out of $16$ possible combinations in the $F_2$ generation, only $1$ plant is double recessive.

Answer: One

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Study the following statements.
(A). Due to incomplete linkage the parental traits are inherited in offsprings.
(B). Due to incomplete linkage new traits occur in offsprings.
(C). X-chromosome of Drosophila males show complete linkage
(D). In Zea mays- color and shape of grain show complete linkage
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Statement (A) is incorrect because parental traits are inherited due to complete linkage. Incomplete linkage leads to the formation of recombinant or new traits along with parental traits, but the inheritance of parental traits itself is a feature of linkage, not specifically incomplete linkage.

Statement (B) is correct because incomplete linkage involves crossing over, which produces new trait combinations (recombinants) in the offspring.

Statement (C) is correct because male Drosophila exhibits complete linkage (no crossing over occurs in male Drosophila).

Statement (D) is incorrect because in Zea mays, the genes for the color and shape of the grain exhibit incomplete linkage, not complete linkage.

Therefore, statements (A) and (D) are incorrect.

Answer: Statements (A) and (D) are incorrect.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Girl of normal vision whose father was colorblind marries a man of normal vision whose father was also colourblind. The sons of this marriage would be
questions.solution:
Color blindness is an X-linked recessive trait.

Let $X^C$ be the normal allele and $X^c$ be the colorblind allele.

The girl has normal vision but her father was colorblind ($X^c Y$). She must have inherited the $X^c$ chromosome from her father, making her genotype $X^C X^c$ (carrier).

The man has normal vision, so his genotype is $X^C Y$. The fact that his father was colorblind does not affect his genotype, as he inherits the Y chromosome from his father.

When they marry ($X^C X^c \times X^C Y$), the sons inherit the Y chromosome from the father and one of the X chromosomes from the mother.

The mother produces two types of gametes containing X chromosomes: $X^C$ and $X^c$ in equal proportion.

Therefore, the sons will have genotypes $X^C Y$ (normal vision) and $X^c Y$ (colorblind) in a $1:1$ ratio.

Thus, $50\%$ of the sons will be colorblind.

Answer: $50\%$ colourblind

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
How many of the following genotypes are representing tall plants with round seeds?
TTrr, Ttrr, TtRr, TTRr, ttrr, TtRR
questions.solution:
In pea plants, the allele for tallness ($T$) is dominant over dwarfness ($t$), and the allele for round seeds ($R$) is dominant over wrinkled seeds ($r$).

For a plant to be tall with round seeds, its genotype must contain at least one dominant allele for both traits, which means it must be of the form $T\_R\_$.

Analyzing the given genotypes:
$TTrr$: Tall, wrinkled
$Ttrr$: Tall, wrinkled
$TtRr$: Tall, round
$TTRr$: Tall, round
$ttrr$: Dwarf, wrinkled
$TtRR$: Tall, round

There are three genotypes ($TtRr$, $TTRr$, and $TtRR$) that represent tall plants with round seeds.

Answer: Three

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following is a mismatch?
options: Turner's syndrome - Females with poor sexual development
questions.solution:
Down's syndrome is caused by the presence of an additional copy of chromosome number 21 (trisomy of 21), which is an autosomal aneuploidy.

Klinefelter's syndrome is caused by the presence of an additional copy of the X-chromosome resulting in a karyotype of 47, XXY. The XO complement is seen in Turner's syndrome. Thus, this is a mismatch.

Haemophilia is a sex-linked recessive disease, which shows its transmission from an unaffected carrier female to some of the male progeny.

Turner's syndrome is caused by the absence of one of the X chromosomes, i.e., 45 with XO. Such females are sterile as ovaries are rudimentary and they have poor sexual development.

Answer: Klinefelter's syndrome - XO complement

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Match List I with List II
List I (Organism)List II (Chromosome number)
(A). Mus musculus(I). 01
(B). Homo sapiens(II). 10
(C). Arabidopsis thaliana(III). 40
(D). E. coli(IV). 46
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
The chromosome number for the given organisms are as follows:

Mus musculus (House mouse) has $40$ chromosomes ($2n = 40$).

Homo sapiens (Human) has $46$ chromosomes ($2n = 46$).

Arabidopsis thaliana has $10$ chromosomes ($2n = 10$).

E. coli is a prokaryote and has $1$ chromosome.

Therefore, the correct matching is (A) - (III), (B) - (IV), (C) - (II), (D) - (I).

Answer: (A) - (III), (B) - (IV), (C) - (II), (D) - (I)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Read the following statement with respect to Phenylketonuria (PKU) and select the correct option.
(A). It is an inborn disorder caused due to dominant gene.
(B). When recessive genes are present in homozygous condition, Phenylalanine hydroxylase enzyme is not produced.
(C). This enzyme is essential for conversion of amino acid tyrosine into phenylanine
options: Statement A, B are correct and C is incorrect
Statement B is correct and A and C are incorrect
Statement A, B and C are incorrect
Statement A, B and C are correct
questions.solution:
Phenylketonuria (PKU) is an inborn error of metabolism that is inherited as an autosomal recessive trait. Thus, statement A is incorrect.

The affected individual lacks the enzyme phenylalanine hydroxylase, which is not produced when the recessive genes are present in a homozygous condition. Thus, statement B is correct.

The enzyme phenylalanine hydroxylase is essential for the conversion of the amino acid phenylalanine into tyrosine, not the other way around. Thus, statement C is incorrect.

Therefore, statement B is correct and A and C are incorrect.

Answer: Statement B is correct and A and C are incorrect

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In mutation breeding, which of the following does NOT represent chemical mutagens?
questions.solution:
Mutation breeding involves inducing mutations to develop new plant varieties with desirable traits. This can be achieved using physical or chemical mutagens.

Chemical mutagens include alkylating agents like methyl methane sulphonate (MMS), base analogues, and other compounds like sodium azide and hydroxylamine that alter DNA structure.

Colchicine is an alkaloid used primarily to induce polyploidy by inhibiting spindle fiber formation during cell division. While it causes genetic changes (changes in chromosome number), it is generally classified as a polyploidizing agent rather than a typical chemical mutagen that induces point mutations or structural chromosomal aberrations.

Therefore, in the context of typical chemical mutagens used for generating variations in breeding, colchicine is not considered one.

Answer: Colchicine

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Linkage of genes in plants was discovered by -----------------.
questions.solution:
Linkage of genes in plants was first discovered by Bateson and Punnett in 1906.

They observed this phenomenon in the sweet pea plant ($Lathyrus$ $odoratus$) while studying the inheritance of flower colour and pollen shape, and proposed the coupling and repulsion hypothesis to explain the lack of independent assortment.

T. H. Morgan later discovered and explained linkage in animals using $Drosophila$ $melanogaster$.

Sutton and Boveri proposed the chromosomal theory of inheritance.

W. Waldeyer coined the term chromosome.

Answer: Bateson and Punnet

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following is NOT an example of complete sex linkage ?
questions.solution:
Complete sex linkage refers to the inheritance of genes located on the non-homologous regions of the X and Y chromosomes, which do not undergo crossing over. Examples of completely sex-linked traits in humans include red-green colour blindness, haemophilia, and myopia.

Incomplete sex linkage involves genes located on the homologous regions of the X and Y chromosomes, which can undergo crossing over. Examples of incompletely sex-linked traits include total colour blindness, retinitis pigmentosa, and nephritis.

Therefore, nephritis is an example of incomplete sex linkage, not complete sex linkage.

Answer: Nephritis

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In a monohybrid cross between pure tall pea plant with pure dwarf pea plant, how many types of genotypes are found in the $F_2$ generation ?
questions.solution:
A pure tall pea plant has the genotype $TT$ and a pure dwarf pea plant has the genotype $tt$.

The cross between them ($TT \times tt$) produces $F_1$ offspring with the genotype $Tt$ (heterozygous tall).

Selfing the $F_1$ generation ($Tt \times Tt$) yields the $F_2$ generation.

The genotypes produced in the $F_2$ generation are $TT$, $Tt$, and $tt$.

Thus, there are $3$ types of genotypes found in the $F_2$ generation.

Answer: 3

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Match List I with List II
List I
(Organisms)		List II
(Chromosome number)
(A). Roundworm(I). 40
(B). Mouse(II). 32
(C). Fruit fly(III). 12
(D). Yeast(IV). 8

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
The correct chromosome numbers (diploid, $2n$) for the given organisms are as follows:

Roundworm (Caenorhabditis elegans) has $12$ chromosomes.
Mouse (Mus musculus) has $40$ chromosomes.
Fruit fly (Drosophila melanogaster) has $8$ chromosomes.
Yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) has $32$ chromosomes.

Therefore, the correct matching is (A) - (III), (B) - (I), (C) - (IV), (D) - (II).

Answer: (A) - (III), (B) - (I), (C) - (IV), (D) - (II)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following is NOT a symptom of Turner's syndrome ?
questions.solution:
Turner's syndrome is a chromosomal disorder in females caused by the absence of one X chromosome ($45$, $X0$).

Common symptoms of Turner's syndrome include a webbed neck, a broad shield-shaped chest with widely spaced nipples, short stature, and a low posterior hairline.

Gynaecomastia refers to the development of breast tissue in males, which is a characteristic feature of Klinefelter's syndrome ($47$, $XXY$), not Turner's syndrome.

Answer: Gynaecomastia

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: The daughter of a colour- blind father and a normal mother marries a normal male. What percentage of male children of this couple will be colour -blind ?
questions.solution:
Colour blindness is an X-linked recessive disorder.

Genotype of the colour-blind father: $X^c Y$

Genotype of the normal mother: $X^C X^C$

The daughter receives the $X^c$ chromosome from her father and the $X^C$ chromosome from her mother. Thus, her genotype is $X^C X^c$ (carrier).

She marries a normal male, whose genotype is $X^C Y$.

The cross between the carrier female ($X^C X^c$) and the normal male ($X^C Y$) produces the following offspring:

Male children receive their X chromosome from the mother and Y chromosome from the father.

The mother produces two types of eggs: $X^C$ and $X^c$ in equal proportions.

Therefore, the male children will be $X^C Y$ (normal) and $X^c Y$ (colour-blind) in a $1:1$ ratio.

Hence, $50\%$ of the male children will be colour-blind.

Answer: 50 %

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: The process of sexual reproduction in animals involves
options: mitosis only and fusion of gametes to form a diploid zygote
meiosis and fusion gametes to form diploid zygote
questions.solution:
Sexual reproduction in animals involves the production of haploid gametes from diploid germ cells through the process of meiosis.

These haploid male and female gametes then fuse during fertilization to form a diploid zygote, which subsequently develops into a new organism.

Therefore, the process essentially involves both meiosis and the fusion of gametes to form a diploid zygote.

Answer: meiosis and fusion gametes to form diploid zygote

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which of the following term is best suited for the process, which involves production of offsprings by the formation and fusion of gametes?
questions.solution:
Amphimixis is the process of sexual reproduction which involves the formation and fusion of male and female gametes to produce offspring.

Apomixis is a type of asexual reproduction that mimics sexual reproduction but occurs without the fusion of gametes.

Apogamy is the development of a sporophyte directly from a gametophyte without fertilization.

Parthenocarpy is the development of fruit without fertilization, resulting in seedless fruits.

Answer: Amphimixis

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Select the correct statement.
options: In Hydra, the bud is developed within the body.
In Spongilla, internal bud develops during favourable season.
Corals reproduce asexually by formation of gametes.
The process of budding is observed in some ascidians.
questions.solution:
In Hydra, the bud develops as an outgrowth on the external surface of the body, which is known as external budding.

In Spongilla (freshwater sponge), internal buds called gemmules are formed during unfavourable environmental conditions to help the organism survive.

Corals reproduce asexually by budding or fragmentation, whereas the formation of gametes is a characteristic of sexual reproduction.

The process of budding is indeed observed in some ascidians (tunicates), where it leads to the formation of colonies.

Answer: The process of budding is observed in some ascidians.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Match List I with List II
List I (Modes of reproduction)List II (Examples)
(A). Binary fission(I). Marchantia
(B). Conidia formation(II). Protosiphon
(C). Gemma formation(III). Diatoms
(D). Budding(IV). Penicillium
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Binary fission is the common mode of asexual reproduction in diatoms.

Conidia formation is the asexual reproduction method in fungi like Penicillium.

Gemma formation occurs in liverworts like Marchantia.

Budding is a mode of asexual reproduction seen in Protosiphon.

Thus, the correct matching is (A) - (III), (B) - (IV), (C) - (I), (D) - (II).

Answer: (A) - (III), (B) - (IV), (C) - (I), (D) - (II)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Given below are two statements.
Statement I - Fusion of two compatible gametes is involved in asexual reproduction.
Statement II - Asexual reproduction results in the production of genetically identical progeny from a single organism which inherits the genes of parents.
In the light of above statements, choose the correct option given below:
options: Both statement I and statement II are correct.
Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
questions.solution:
Statement I is incorrect because the fusion of two compatible gametes is a characteristic of sexual reproduction, not asexual reproduction. Asexual reproduction does not involve the fusion of gametes.

Statement II is correct because asexual reproduction involves a single parent and produces offspring that are genetically and morphologically identical to each other and to the parent, often referred to as clones.

Answer: Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: The most common method of asexual reproduction in unicellular Yeast is --------------.
questions.solution:
Yeast is a unicellular fungus that primarily reproduces asexually through a process called budding. In this process, a small outgrowth or bud develops on the parent cell, which eventually detaches to form a new yeast cell.

Fragmentation is seen in filamentous organisms like Spirogyra.

Binary fission is common in bacteria and Amoeba.

Conidia formation is observed in fungi like Penicillium.

Answer: Budding

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Given below are a few statements about asexual reproduction. Select the INCORRECT statement.
options: It inherits the genes of the parent.
Morphologically identical individuals are called clones
Progeny formed by asexual reproduction differs genetically from its parents
It doesn't involve fusion of two compatible gametes
questions.solution:
Asexual reproduction is a mode of reproduction that involves a single parent and does not involve the fusion of gametes or a change in the number of chromosomes.

The offspring produced by asexual reproduction are exact copies of their single parent, inheriting the exact same genes.

Because these individuals are morphologically and genetically identical to each other and to their parent, they are referred to as clones.

Therefore, the statement that progeny formed by asexual reproduction differs genetically from its parents is incorrect.

Answer: Progeny formed by asexual reproduction differs genetically from its parents

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Identify correct function of following IUDs:
options: Lippes loop - It attracts the macrophages stimulating them to accumulate in the uterine cavity.
Cu-T - It delays pregnancy for longer period.
Cu 7 - Makes the uterus unsuitable for implantation.
questions.solution:
Lippes loop is a non-medicated IUD. It induces a mild inflammatory response in the endometrium, which attracts macrophages and stimulates them to accumulate in the uterine cavity, thereby increasing the phagocytosis of sperms.

Cu-T and Cu 7 are copper-releasing IUDs. The released copper ions suppress sperm motility and the fertilising capacity of sperms.

LNG-20 is a hormone-releasing IUD. It makes the uterus unsuitable for implantation and the cervix hostile to sperms.

Answer: Lippes loop - It attracts the macrophages stimulating them to accumulate in the uterine cavity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which of the following is hormone releasing IUD?
questions.solution:
Intrauterine Devices (IUDs) are classified into three main types:

Non-medicated IUDs: e.g., Lippes loop.

Copper-releasing IUDs: e.g., CuT, Cu7, Multiload 375. These release copper ions which suppress sperm motility and the fertilising capacity of sperms.

Hormone-releasing IUDs: e.g., Progestasert, LNG-20. These release hormones that make the uterus unsuitable for implantation and the cervix hostile to the sperms.

Therefore, LNG-20 is a hormone-releasing IUD.

Answer: LNG - 20

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement (I): One of the physiological devices used as birth control measures is in the form of tablets.
Statement (II): Contraceptive pill 'Saheli' is hormonal and steroidal in nature
In light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true.
Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are false.
Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.
questions.solution:
Statement (I) is true because oral contraceptives (pills) are physiological methods of birth control that are taken in the form of tablets. They alter physiological processes such as inhibiting ovulation and implantation, as well as altering the quality of cervical mucus to prevent the entry of sperms.

Statement (II) is false because the contraceptive pill 'Saheli' is a non-steroidal preparation. It is a once-a-week pill with very few side effects and high contraceptive value.

Therefore, Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.

Answer: Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following is NOT a draw back of intra-uterine devices (IUDs) for birth control?
questions.solution:
Intra-uterine devices (IUDs) are a popular and effective reversible method of contraception.

The common drawbacks or side effects of IUDs include spontaneous expulsion, occasional haemorrhage (bleeding), and the risk of pelvic infection.

IUDs do not cause permanent infertility; normal fertility is restored promptly after the removal of the device.

Therefore, the statement that it leads to permanent infertility is incorrect and is not a drawback of IUDs.

Answer: It leads to permanent infertility.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Match List I with List II.
List IList II
(A). Vaults(I). Entry of sperm through cervix is blocked.
(B). IUDs(II). Blockage of vas deferens
(C). Vasectomy(III). Phagocytosis of sperms within the uterus
(D). Tubectomy(IV). Blockage of fallopian tube
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Vaults are barrier methods of contraception which are inserted into the female reproductive tract to cover the cervix, thus blocking the entry of sperms through the cervix.

IUDs (Intra Uterine Devices) are inserted by doctors or expert nurses in the uterus through the vagina. They increase phagocytosis of sperms within the uterus.

Vasectomy is a surgical sterilization method in males where a small part of the vas deferens is removed or tied up, blocking sperm transport.

Tubectomy is a surgical sterilization method in females where a small part of the fallopian tube is removed or tied up, blocking the transport of the ovum and preventing fertilization.

Therefore, the correct matching is (A) - (I), (B) - (III), (C) - (II), (D) - (IV).

Answer: (A) - (I), (B) - (III), (C) - (II), (D) - (IV)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Match List I with List II
List I
(Contraceptive aids.)		List II
(Mode of action.)
(A). Lippes loop(I). Make the uterus unsuitable for implantation and cervix hostile to the sperms.
(B). Cu-T(II). Attracts the macrophages which increase phagocytosis of sperms.
(C). LNG-20(III). Blocks the entry of sperms through the cervix.
(D). Vaults(IV). Suppress sperm mobility and fertilizing capacity of sperms.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Lippes loop is a non-medicated IUD which increases phagocytosis of sperms within the uterus by attracting macrophages.

Cu-T is a copper-releasing IUD. The released copper ions suppress sperm motility and the fertilizing capacity of sperms.

LNG-20 is a hormone-releasing IUD which makes the uterus unsuitable for implantation and the cervix hostile to sperms.

Vaults are barrier methods of contraception which block the entry of sperms through the cervix.

Thus, the correct matching is (A) - (II), (B) - (IV), (C) - (I), (D) - (III).

Answer: (A) - (II), (B) - (IV), (C) - (I), (D) - (III)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: ZIFT, an assisted reproductive technology, is transfer of ________.
options: ovum collected from the donor to fallopian tube of the another female.
embryo into the uterus of the female.
externally fertilized ovum to the fallopian tube of the female.
sperms directly into the fallopian tube of female.
questions.solution:
ZIFT stands for Zygote Intra Fallopian Transfer.

In this technique, the ovum is fertilized externally (in vitro fertilization) to form a zygote. The zygote or early embryo (up to 8 blastomeres) is then transferred into the fallopian tube of the female to complete its further development.

Transfer of an ovum collected from a donor into the fallopian tube of another female is called GIFT (Gamete Intra Fallopian Transfer).

Transfer of an embryo with more than 8 blastomeres into the uterus is called IUT (Intra Uterine Transfer).

Answer: externally fertilized ovum to the fallopian tube of the female.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Complete the following analogy:
Cu-T : Suppress sperm motility
LNG-20 : ___________
options: Makes cervix hostile to the sperm
Alters the quality of cervical mucus
questions.solution:
Cu-T is a copper-releasing Intra Uterine Device (IUD). The copper ions released suppress sperm motility and the fertilising capacity of sperms.

LNG-20 is a hormone-releasing IUD. Hormone-releasing IUDs make the uterus unsuitable for implantation and the cervix hostile to the sperms.

Answer: Makes cervix hostile to the sperm

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Identify the INCORRECT match
options: Diaphragm $\rightarrow$ Made of rubber to cover penis
questions.solution:
LNG-20 is a hormone-releasing IUD.

Multiload 375 is a copper-releasing IUD.

Lippes loop is a non-medicated IUD made of plastic in a double 'S' shape.

Diaphragms, cervical caps, and vaults are barrier methods made of rubber that are inserted into the female reproductive tract to cover the cervix during coitus. They do not cover the penis. Condoms are used to cover the penis.

Therefore, the match for Diaphragm is incorrect.

Answer: Diaphragm $\rightarrow$ Made of rubber to cover penis

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Identify the hormone releasing Intra Uterine Device from the following.
questions.solution:
Intra Uterine Devices (IUDs) are classified into three main types:

Non-medicated IUDs: Lippes loop.

Copper releasing IUDs: CuT, Cu7, Multiload 375.

Hormone releasing IUDs: Progestasert, LNG-20.

Therefore, LNG-20 is a hormone releasing IUD.

Answer: LNG-20

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which of the following contraceptive devices is usually used only by males?
questions.solution:
Condoms are barrier methods of contraception made of thin rubber or latex sheath. While both male and female condoms exist, male condoms are the most widely used contraceptive device by males.

Diaphragms are barrier methods inserted into the female reproductive tract to cover the cervix.

Lippes Loop is an Intra Uterine Device (IUD) inserted into the female uterus.

Oral pills are hormonal contraceptive methods used by females.

Answer: Condom

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Diosgenin is primarily used in the manufacturing of------
questions.solution:
Diosgenin is a phytosteroid sapogenin extracted from the tubers of Dioscorea species (wild yam). It serves as a crucial precursor for the semi-synthesis of various steroid hormones, most notably progesterone. Progesterone and its derivatives are key active ingredients in oral contraceptives. Therefore, diosgenin is primarily used in the manufacturing of birth control pills.

Answer: birth control pills

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following is the activator for enzyme hexokinase in glycolysis?
questions.solution:
Hexokinase is the first enzyme in the glycolysis pathway, which catalyzes the phosphorylation of glucose to glucose-6-phosphate using ATP.

The true substrate for hexokinase is not free ATP, but the $\text{Mg}^{++}$-ATP complex. The $\text{Mg}^{++}$ ion shields the negative charges of the phosphate groups in ATP, making the terminal phosphorus atom an easier target for nucleophilic attack by the hydroxyl group of glucose.

Therefore, $\text{Mg}^{++}$ acts as an essential cofactor and activator for the enzyme hexokinase.

Answer: $\text{Mg}^{++}$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: What is the site of Krebs cycle in mitochondria?
questions.solution:
The Krebs cycle, also known as the citric acid cycle or tricarboxylic acid (TCA) cycle, occurs in the mitochondrial matrix.

The mitochondrial matrix contains all the necessary enzymes for the Krebs cycle, except for succinate dehydrogenase, which is located in the inner mitochondrial membrane.

The outer membrane is involved in the transport of molecules.

Oxysomes (F0-F1 particles) are present on the inner mitochondrial membrane and are involved in ATP synthesis.

Cristae are the infoldings of the inner mitochondrial membrane that house the electron transport chain.

Answer: Matrix

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Respiratory quotient is represented as _______
questions.solution:
Respiratory quotient (RQ) is defined as the ratio of the volume of carbon dioxide evolved (released) to the volume of oxygen consumed during respiration.

$\text{RQ} = \dfrac{\text{Volume of CO}_2\ \text{released}}{\text{Volume of O}_2\ \text{consumed}}$

Answer: $\dfrac{\text{CO}_2\ \text{released}}{\text{O}_2\ \text{consumed}}$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: How many ATP molecules are utilized in the glycolysis of a glucose molecule?
questions.solution:
Glycolysis involves an energy investment phase where ATP is consumed.

One ATP molecule is utilized during the conversion of glucose to glucose-6-phosphate, catalyzed by the enzyme hexokinase.

Another ATP molecule is utilized during the conversion of fructose-6-phosphate to fructose-1,6-bisphosphate, catalyzed by the enzyme phosphofructokinase.

Therefore, a total of $2$ ATP molecules are utilized in the glycolysis of a single glucose molecule.

Answer: $2$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Identify the INCORRECT statement regarding glycolysis. There is _________.
questions.solution:
Glycolysis is the process of partial oxidation of glucose to form two molecules of pyruvic acid.

During the preparatory phase of glycolysis, two molecules of ATP are consumed.

During the payoff phase, four molecules of ATP are produced, resulting in a net gain of two ATP molecules.

No carbon dioxide is released during glycolysis. Decarboxylation occurs later in the link reaction and Krebs cycle.

Thus, the statement regarding the formation of $\text{CO}_2$ is incorrect.

Answer: formation of $\text{CO}_2$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Given below are two statements.
Statement I - Protons are channeled from inner chamber of mitochondria into outer chamber by $\text{F}_0$ part of oxysome during ETS.
Statement II - Cytochrome C is a small, iron containing protein tightly associated with inner membrane.
In the light of above statements, select the correct option given below:
options: Both statement I and statement II are correct
Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
questions.solution:
Statement I is incorrect because the $\text{F}_0$ part of the oxysome (ATP synthase) forms a channel through which protons move from the intermembrane space (outer chamber) to the matrix (inner chamber) down the electrochemical proton gradient during ATP synthesis, not from the inner chamber to the outer chamber.

Statement II is incorrect because Cytochrome c is a small protein attached loosely to the outer surface of the inner mitochondrial membrane, not tightly associated. It acts as a mobile carrier for the transfer of electrons between complex III and IV.

Therefore, both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.

Answer: Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: The movement of protons during ATP synthesis in mitochondria takes place from outer chamber into inner chamber by $F_0$ part of oxysome present in the
questions.solution:
The oxysome or ATP synthase consists of two major components, $F_0$ and $F_1$.

The $F_0$ portion is an integral membrane protein complex embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane. It forms a transmembrane channel that facilitates the diffusion of protons from the intermembrane space (outer chamber) back into the mitochondrial matrix (inner chamber).

The $F_1$ portion is a peripheral membrane protein complex situated on the matrix side of the inner mitochondrial membrane, where it catalyzes the synthesis of ATP from ADP and inorganic phosphate.

Answer: inner mitochondrial membrane.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: How many ATP molecules are produced thorough ETS, out of 38 molecules produced after complete oxidation of one glucose molecule?
questions.solution:
During the complete oxidation of one glucose molecule, a total of $38$ ATP molecules are produced.

Substrate-level phosphorylation (SLP) directly yields ATP without the involvement of the Electron Transport System (ETS). The ATP produced via SLP is:
Glycolysis: $2$ ATP
Krebs cycle: $2$ ATP (one per turn, two turns per glucose)
Total ATP from SLP = $4$ ATP.

The remaining ATP molecules are generated through oxidative phosphorylation via the ETS from reduced coenzymes (NADH and FADH$_2$).
Total ATP from ETS = Total ATP $-$ ATP from SLP
Total ATP from ETS = $38 - 4 = 34$ ATP.

Alternatively, calculating from reduced coenzymes:
Glycolysis: $2$ NADH $\rightarrow 6$ ATP
Link reaction: $2$ NADH $\rightarrow 6$ ATP
Krebs cycle: $6$ NADH $\rightarrow 18$ ATP and $2$ FADH$_2$ $\rightarrow 4$ ATP
Total ATP from ETS = $6 + 6 + 18 + 4 = 34$ ATP.

Answer: $34$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: R.Q. is ratio of volume of
questions.solution:
Respiratory Quotient (R.Q.) is defined as the ratio of the volume of carbon dioxide evolved to the volume of oxygen consumed during respiration.

$\text{R.Q.} = \dfrac{\text{Volume of CO}_2 \text{ released}}{\text{Volume of O}_2 \text{ consumed}}$

Answer: $\text{CO}_2$ released to $\text{O}_2$ consumed

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: The process in which food is oxidized and ATP is generated is called ________ .
questions.solution:
Cellular respiration is the biochemical process in which the chemical energy of food (glucose) is released through oxidation and captured in the form of ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate).

Breathing is the physical process of inhaling oxygen and exhaling carbon dioxide.

External respiration refers to the exchange of gases between the alveoli of the lungs and the blood.

Internal respiration refers to the exchange of gases between the blood and the body cells.

Answer: cellular respiration

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Find the CORRECT statements from the following and choose the correct option.
(A). Glycolysis (EMP pathway) is a common path for aerobic and anaerobic respiration.
(B). Glycolysis occurs in mitochondria.
(C). Glycolysis involves the breakdown of glucose molecule into two pyruvic acid molecules.
(D). During Glycolysis, ATP is produced by oxidative phosphorylation.
questions.solution:
Statement (A) is correct. Glycolysis, also known as the EMP pathway, is the first stage of cellular respiration and is common to both aerobic and anaerobic respiration.

Statement (B) is incorrect. Glycolysis occurs in the cytoplasm of the cell, not in the mitochondria.

Statement (C) is correct. During glycolysis, one molecule of glucose (a 6-carbon compound) is partially oxidized to form two molecules of pyruvic acid (a 3-carbon compound).

Statement (D) is incorrect. During glycolysis, ATP is produced through substrate-level phosphorylation, whereas oxidative phosphorylation occurs later during the electron transport system in the mitochondria.

Therefore, only statements (A) and (C) are correct.

Answer: (A) and (C) only.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: During lactic acid fermentation, pyruvic acid is reduced to lactic acid using one proton and two electrons from ________________.
questions.solution:
During lactic acid fermentation, pyruvic acid is reduced to lactic acid by the enzyme lactate dehydrogenase.

The reducing agent for this reaction is $\text{NADH} + \text{H}^+$, which is produced during the oxidation steps of glycolysis.

In this process, $\text{NADH} + \text{H}^+$ donates electrons and a proton to pyruvic acid, getting reoxidized to $\text{NAD}^+$, which is then reused in glycolysis.

Answer: $\text{NADH} + \text{H}^+$ produced during glycolysis.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Where does the end product of Glycolysis undergoes aerobic oxidation in eukaryotes?
questions.solution:
The end product of glycolysis is pyruvic acid (pyruvate), which is formed in the cytoplasm.

Under aerobic conditions in eukaryotes, pyruvic acid is transported into the mitochondria.

Within the mitochondrial matrix, pyruvic acid undergoes oxidative decarboxylation catalyzed by the enzyme pyruvate dehydrogenase to form acetyl-CoA, which then enters the Krebs cycle for complete aerobic oxidation.

Therefore, the aerobic oxidation of the end product of glycolysis occurs in the mitochondrial matrix.

Answer: Mitochondrial matrix.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In Krebs' cycle, GTP is produced during the conversion of ___.
questions.solution:
In the Krebs cycle, the conversion of succinyl CoA to succinic acid is catalyzed by the enzyme succinyl-CoA synthetase.

During this reaction, the cleavage of the high-energy thioester bond of succinyl CoA releases energy, which is used to phosphorylate a molecule of GDP to GTP. This process is an example of substrate-level phosphorylation.

The GTP produced can subsequently transfer its terminal phosphate group to ADP to form ATP.

Therefore, GTP is produced during the conversion of succinyl CoA to succinic acid.

Answer: succinyl CoA to succinic acid.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement (I): $\text{NADH}_2$ ($\text{NADH}+\text{H}^+$) and $\text{FADH}_2$ that are produced during glycolysis, connecting link reaction and Krebs cycle, are oxidised with the help of various electron carriers and enzymes.
Statement (II): These carriers and enzymes are arranged in the cytoplasm in the form of various complexes as complex I, II, III, VI and V.
In light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true.
Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are false.
Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.
questions.solution:
Statement (I) is true because $\text{NADH} + \text{H}^+$ and $\text{FADH}_2$ produced during glycolysis, the link reaction, and the Krebs cycle are oxidised by the electron transport system (ETS) which consists of various electron carriers and enzymes.

Statement (II) is false because the electron carriers and enzymes of the ETS are located in the inner mitochondrial membrane, not in the cytoplasm. Furthermore, they are arranged in the form of complexes I, II, III, IV, and V, not VI.

Answer: Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement (I): There is accumulation of lactic acid in skeletal muscles during vigorous exercise.
Statement (II): Lactic acid is reconverted into pyruvic acid during resting stage of skeletal muscles.
In light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are correct.
Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are incorrect.
Statement (I) is correct but Statement (II) is incorrect.
Statement (I) is incorrect but Statement (II) is correct.
questions.solution:
During vigorous exercise, the oxygen demand of skeletal muscles exceeds the oxygen supply. To meet the energy requirements, skeletal muscles undergo anaerobic respiration, where pyruvic acid is reduced to lactic acid by the enzyme lactate dehydrogenase. This leads to the accumulation of lactic acid, causing muscle fatigue. Thus, Statement (I) is correct.

During the resting stage, when adequate oxygen becomes available, the accumulated lactic acid is oxidized back into pyruvic acid. A portion of this pyruvic acid is oxidized in the muscles to generate ATP, while the rest is transported to the liver to be converted into glucose (Cori cycle). Thus, Statement (II) is also correct.

Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are correct.

Answer: Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are correct.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following conversion in glycolysis shows formation of water molecules from the substrate?
questions.solution:
In glycolysis, the conversion of 2-phosphoglycerate to phosphoenolpyruvate is catalyzed by the enzyme enolase.

This step involves the elimination of a water molecule (dehydration) from 2-phosphoglycerate to form the enol structure of phosphoenolpyruvate.

Therefore, the formation of a water molecule occurs during the conversion of 2-phosphoglycerate to phosphoenolpyruvate.

Answer: 2 Phosphoglycerate $\rightarrow$ 2 Phosphoenolpyruvate

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Select the set of correct statements regarding ETS in aerobic respiration.
A. $F_1$ catalyses the synthesis of ATP from ADP.
B. Protons are channeled from outer chamber of mitochondria into inner chamber by $F_0$ part of oxysome.
C. Cytochrome C acts as mobile electron carrier transferring electrons from Complex V and IV.
D. Cytochrome C is a small, calcium containing protein tightly associated with inner membrane.
E. Cytochrome C acts as mobile electron carrier transferring electrons from complex III to IV.
Select the correct option
questions.solution:
Statement A is correct. The $F_1$ headpiece of ATP synthase contains the catalytic site for the synthesis of ATP from ADP and inorganic phosphate.

Statement B is correct. The $F_0$ part of the oxysome (ATP synthase) forms a transmembrane channel that facilitates the diffusion of protons from the intermembrane space (outer chamber) to the mitochondrial matrix (inner chamber).

Statement C is incorrect. Cytochrome c transfers electrons between Complex III (Cytochrome $bc_1$ complex) and Complex IV (Cytochrome c oxidase), not Complex V.

Statement D is incorrect. Cytochrome c is a small protein attached loosely to the outer surface of the inner mitochondrial membrane, and it contains iron (in its heme group), not calcium.

Statement E is correct. Cytochrome c acts as a mobile electron carrier transferring electrons from Complex III to Complex IV.

Therefore, statements A, B, and E are correct.

Answer: A, B and E only

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Oxidation of one $\text{NADH} + \text{H}^+$ leads to production of _________ ATP molecules.
questions.solution:
During oxidative phosphorylation in the electron transport system (ETS), the oxidation of one molecule of $\text{NADH} + \text{H}^+$ yields $3$ molecules of ATP.

Similarly, the oxidation of one molecule of $\text{FADH}_2$ produces $2$ molecules of ATP.

Answer: three

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: The ultimate electron acceptor in ETS in aerobic respiration is ___________ and it reacts with protons to form ________.
questions.solution:
In the electron transport system (ETS) of aerobic respiration, the final or ultimate electron acceptor is molecular oxygen.

Oxygen accepts electrons from cytochrome c oxidase (Complex IV) and reacts with protons from the mitochondrial matrix to form metabolic water.

The reaction is $\dfrac{1}{2} O_2 + 2H^+ + 2e^- \rightarrow H_2O$.

Answer: Molecular oxygen, metabolic water

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Glycolysis occurs in _____________
questions.solution:
Glycolysis is the metabolic pathway that converts glucose into pyruvate. The enzymes required for this process are present in the cytoplasm of the cell.

Therefore, glycolysis occurs in the cytoplasm.

Answer: cytoplasm

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Total number of ATP molecules utilised in one glycolytic pathway is _________.
questions.solution:
Glycolysis is the metabolic pathway that converts glucose into pyruvate.

During the energy investment phase of glycolysis, ATP is consumed in two specific reactions:

1. Phosphorylation of glucose to glucose-6-phosphate, catalyzed by the enzyme hexokinase, utilizes 1 ATP molecule.

2. Phosphorylation of fructose-6-phosphate to fructose-1,6-bisphosphate, catalyzed by the enzyme phosphofructokinase, utilizes 1 ATP molecule.

Therefore, a total of 2 ATP molecules are utilized in one glycolytic pathway.

Answer: 2

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Identify the step, in which phosphorylation occurs with the help of inorganic phosphate and not ATP.
questions.solution:
In glycolysis, the conversion of Glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate to 1,3-bisphosphoglycerate is catalyzed by the enzyme glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase.

During this step, oxidation of Glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate occurs, and inorganic phosphate ($H_3PO_4$ or $P_i$) is added to form 1,3-bisphosphoglycerate. ATP is not utilized in this phosphorylation step.

In the conversion of Glucose to Glucose-6-phosphate, ATP is used as the phosphate donor.

In the conversions of 1,3-bisphosphoglycerate to 3-phosphoglycerate and Phosphoenol pyruvate to Pyruvic acid, ATP is synthesized (substrate-level phosphorylation).

Answer: Glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate (3C) $\rightarrow$ 1,3 bisphosphoglycerate (3C)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In glycolysis, which enzyme requires $\text{Zn}^{2+}$ as cofactor?
questions.solution:
In the process of glycolysis, specific enzymes require metal ions or coenzymes as cofactors for their catalytic activity.

Hexokinase and Phosphofructokinase are kinase enzymes that catalyze the transfer of phosphate groups from ATP. They typically require $Mg^{2+}$ as a cofactor to stabilize the negative charges of ATP.

Glyceraldehyde phosphate dehydrogenase is an oxidoreductase enzyme that utilizes the coenzyme $NAD^+$ to catalyze its reaction and does not require a metal ion cofactor.

Aldolase, specifically Class II aldolase, is a metalloenzyme that requires a divalent metal ion such as $Zn^{2+}$ as a cofactor to stabilize the reaction intermediates during the cleavage of fructose 1,6-bisphosphate into glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate and dihydroxyacetone phosphate.

Thus, aldolase is the enzyme that requires $Zn^{2+}$ as a cofactor.

Answer: Aldolase

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which of the following coenzyme is NOT directly required in any of the steps of the Kreb's cycle?
questions.solution:
The Krebs cycle involves a series of oxidation-reduction and substrate-level phosphorylation reactions.

NAD$^+$ is required as a coenzyme for the enzymes isocitrate dehydrogenase, $\alpha$-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase, and malate dehydrogenase.

FAD is required as a coenzyme for succinate dehydrogenase.

Coenzyme A (CoA) is required by the $\alpha$-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase complex to form succinyl-CoA.

Coenzyme Q (Ubiquinone) is a mobile electron carrier in the electron transport chain (ETC) located in the inner mitochondrial membrane and is not directly involved in any of the steps of the Krebs cycle.

Answer: Coenzyme Q

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which of the following processes adds $\text{CO}_2$ to the atmosphere?
questions.solution:
Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize nutrients from carbon dioxide and water, thereby removing $\text{CO}_2$ from the atmosphere.

Respiration is a metabolic process in living organisms involving the production of energy, typically with the intake of oxygen and the release of carbon dioxide, thus adding $\text{CO}_2$ to the atmosphere.

Carboxylation is a chemical reaction in which a carboxylic acid group is produced by treating a substrate with carbon dioxide, which consumes $\text{CO}_2$.

Nitrification is the biological oxidation of ammonia to nitrite followed by the oxidation of the nitrite to nitrate, which does not release $\text{CO}_2$.

Therefore, respiration is the process that adds $\text{CO}_2$ to the atmosphere.

Answer: Respiration

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: $\alpha$-Ketoglutarate and Oxaloacetate act as precursors in the synthesis of ______.
questions.solution:
$\alpha$-Ketoglutarate is a key intermediate in the Krebs cycle and serves as the precursor for the synthesis of the amino acid glutamic acid (glutamate) through the process of reductive amination or transamination.

Oxaloacetate, another intermediate of the Krebs cycle, acts as the precursor for the synthesis of aspartic acid (aspartate) via transamination.

Thus, $\alpha$-Ketoglutarate and Oxaloacetate act as precursors in the synthesis of Glutamic acid and Aspartic acid, respectively.

Answer: Glutamic acid, Aspartic acid

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: If glucose is incompletely oxidized to ethyl alcohol in the absence of oxygen, the RQ will be ______.
questions.solution:
The chemical equation for the incomplete oxidation of glucose to ethyl alcohol in the absence of oxygen (anaerobic respiration) is given by:

$C_6H_{12}O_6 \rightarrow 2C_2H_5OH + 2CO_2 + \text{Energy}$

The respiratory quotient (RQ) is the ratio of the volume of carbon dioxide evolved to the volume of oxygen consumed during respiration.

$RQ = \dfrac{\text{Volume of } CO_2 \text{ evolved}}{\text{Volume of } O_2 \text{ consumed}}$

In this process, $2$ molecules of $CO_2$ are evolved while $0$ molecules of $O_2$ are consumed.

$RQ = \dfrac{2}{0} = \infty$

Thus, the RQ is infinite.

Answer: infinite

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which cytochrome acts as a mobile electron carrier, transferring electrons between complex III and IV during ETS?
questions.solution:
Cytochrome c is a small protein attached to the outer surface of the inner mitochondrial membrane.

It acts as a mobile carrier for the transfer of electrons between complex III (cytochrome bc$_1$ complex) and complex IV (cytochrome c oxidase complex).

Therefore, cytochrome c is the correct mobile electron carrier.

Answer: Cytochrome c

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which enzyme is responsible for phosphorylation of glucose with ATP?
questions.solution:
The first step of glycolysis involves the phosphorylation of glucose to form glucose-6-phosphate.

This reaction is catalyzed by the enzyme hexokinase, which uses ATP as the phosphate donor.

Phosphofructokinase catalyzes the phosphorylation of fructose-6-phosphate to fructose-1,6-bisphosphate.

Aldolase catalyzes the cleavage of fructose-1,6-bisphosphate into two three-carbon sugars.

Phosphohexose isomerase catalyzes the isomerization of glucose-6-phosphate to fructose-6-phosphate.

Answer: Hexokinase

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: How many glucose molecules are utilized if the cell produces 68 pyruvic acid molecules during glycolysis?
questions.solution:
During glycolysis, one molecule of glucose is broken down to produce two molecules of pyruvic acid.

Number of glucose molecules utilized = $\dfrac{\text{Number of pyruvic acid molecules produced}}{2}$

Number of glucose molecules utilized = $\dfrac{68}{2} = 34$

Answer: $34$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Glycolysis ends in formation of ________ .
questions.solution:
Glycolysis is the metabolic pathway that converts glucose into two molecules of pyruvic acid.

It occurs in the cytoplasm of the cell and is the first step of both aerobic and anaerobic respiration.

Lactic acid, acetaldehyde, and ethanol are products formed during fermentation (anaerobic respiration), which takes place after glycolysis.

Therefore, glycolysis ends in the formation of pyruvic acid.

Answer: pyruvic acid

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: During ETS, when ubiquinol is oxidized by the cytochrome $bc_1$ complex, the electrons released are transferred to ____.
questions.solution:
In the mitochondrial electron transport system (ETS), ubiquinol is oxidized by the cytochrome $bc_1$ complex (Complex III).

During this oxidation, the electrons are transferred to cytochrome c, which is a small mobile protein attached to the outer surface of the inner mitochondrial membrane.

Cytochrome c then acts as a mobile carrier to transfer these electrons between Complex III and Complex IV (cytochrome c oxidase complex, which contains cytochromes a and $a_3$).

Answer: Cytochrome C

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: During aerobic respiration, the total number of ATP formed from one glucose molecule through substrate level phosphorylation is ----------------------.
questions.solution:
SIn aerobic respiration, ATP is synthesized from a single molecule of glucose through two distinct pathways: substrate-level phosphorylation and oxidative phosphorylation.

In the classical balance sheet of aerobic respiration, the complete oxidation of one glucose molecule yields a net total of $38$ ATP molecules. This total consists of $34$ ATP molecules produced via oxidative phosphorylation in the electron transport chain and $4$ ATP molecules produced via substrate-level phosphorylation.

The breakdown of ATP formed directly by substrate-level phosphorylation is as follows:

In glycolysis, which takes place in the cytoplasm, a gross total of $4$ ATP molecules are synthesized during the payoff phase. However, $2$ ATP molecules are consumed during the initial preparatory phase, leaving a net gain of $2$ ATP molecules.

In the Krebs cycle, which takes place in the mitochondrial matrix, $1$ GTP molecule (equivalent to $1$ ATP) is generated directly per turn during the conversion of succinyl-CoA to succinate. Since one molecule of glucose gives rise to two molecules of acetyl-CoA, the Krebs cycle operates twice per glucose molecule, resulting in the production of $2$ ATP molecules.

Summing up the net contributions from both stages, the total number of net ATP molecules formed through substrate-level phosphorylation is:
$2\text{ (from glycolysis)} + 2\text{ (from Krebs cycle)} = 4\text{ ATP}$

Answer: $4$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: The formation of ATP by oxidative phosphorylation takes place in ____.
questions.solution:
Oxidative phosphorylation is the process in which ATP is formed as a result of the transfer of electrons from NADH or FADH$_2$ to O$_2$ by a series of electron carriers. This process takes place in the Electron Transport System (ETS) located in the inner mitochondrial membrane.

In glycolysis and the Krebs cycle, ATP is formed through substrate-level phosphorylation.

Fermentation is an anaerobic process that does not involve oxidative phosphorylation.

Answer: ETS

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement (I): The rate of glycolysis is controlled by ATP consumption, $NAD^++H^+$ regeneration and enzymes like hexokinase, pyruvate kinase etc.
Statement (II): It is also controlled by hormones like glucagon, epinephrine and insulin.
In light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are correct.
Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are incorrect.
Statement (I) is correct but Statement (II) is incorrect.
Statement (I) is incorrect but Statement (II) is correct.
questions.solution:
Statement (I) is correct because the rate of glycolysis is fundamentally controlled by the cellular energy charge, including ATP consumption and the continuous need for $NAD^+$ regeneration from $NADH + H^+$. The process is strictly regulated by key allosteric enzymes such as hexokinase, phosphofructokinase, and pyruvate kinase.

Statement (II) is correct because glycolysis is also subject to hormonal regulation to maintain systemic blood glucose homeostasis. Hormones like insulin upregulate glycolysis by promoting the activation of regulatory enzymes, whereas glucagon and epinephrine generally inhibit glycolysis in the liver via phosphorylation cascades.

Therefore, both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are correct.

Answer: Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are correct.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Given below are two statements regarding anaerobic respiration in the muscles.
Statement (I): In muscles, NADH+H$^+$ produced during glycolysis is reoxidised to NAD.
Statement (II): One proton and two electrons produced, which are donated to pyruvic acid which yields lactic acid leading to muscle fatigue.
In light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are correct.
Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are incorrect.
Statement (I) is correct but Statement (II) is incorrect.
Statement (I) is incorrect but Statement (II) is correct.
questions.solution:
Statement (I) is correct because during anaerobic respiration in muscle cells (lactic acid fermentation), the NADH + H$^+$ produced during glycolysis is reoxidised to NAD$^+$ to ensure a continuous supply of NAD$^+$ for glycolysis to proceed.

Statement (II) is incorrect because during the reduction of pyruvic acid to lactic acid, NADH + H$^+$ donates two protons ($2$H$^+$) and two electrons ($2$e$^-$) to pyruvic acid, not one proton and two electrons. The reaction is catalysed by lactate dehydrogenase.

Answer: Statement (I) is correct but Statement (II) is incorrect.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: The protons released during the oxidation of NADH+H$^+$ and FADH$_2$ in mitochondria follow the paths given below EXCEPT:
options: Protons are released into intermembrane space.
They are channelized into the inner chamber by $\text{F}_0$ part of oxysome present in the inner membrane.
These protons react with reduced molecular oxygen to form water molecule.
This water molecule evaporates through the stomata.
questions.solution:
During the electron transport chain in mitochondria, the oxidation of NADH+H$^+$ and FADH$_2$ releases protons and electrons.

The protons are pumped from the mitochondrial matrix into the intermembrane space, creating a proton gradient.

These protons then move back into the inner chamber (matrix) through the F$_0$ channel of the ATP synthase (oxysome) present in the inner mitochondrial membrane, driving ATP synthesis.

In the matrix, the protons combine with the terminal electron acceptor, molecular oxygen, and electrons to form metabolic water.

The metabolic water formed is generally utilized within the cell for various biochemical processes and does not specifically evaporate through the stomata as its primary fate. Therefore, the statement about the water molecule evaporating through the stomata is the exception.

Answer: This water molecule evaporates through the stomata.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q:
Given below are four respiratory enzymes. Arrange them in the decreasing order of number of carbon atoms present in the substrates they act on.
(A). Fumarase
(B). Aconitase
(C). Enolase
(D). $\alpha$ - keto glutarate dehydrogenase
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Aconitase acts on citrate, which is a 6-carbon compound.

$\alpha$-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase acts on $\alpha$-ketoglutarate, which is a 5-carbon compound.

Fumarase acts on fumarate, which is a 4-carbon compound.

Enolase acts on 2-phosphoglycerate, which is a 3-carbon compound.

The decreasing order of the number of carbon atoms in their substrates is 6C, 5C, 4C, 3C.

This corresponds to the sequence (B), (D), (A), (C).

Answer: (B), (D), (A), (C).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which one of the following intermediate metabolites is common for aerobic break down of glucose, aminoacid and fatty acids?
questions.solution:
Carbohydrates are broken down into glucose, which undergoes glycolysis to form pyruvic acid. Pyruvic acid is then oxidatively decarboxylated to form acetyl CoA.

Fats are broken down into fatty acids and glycerol. Fatty acids undergo $\beta$-oxidation to directly form acetyl CoA.

Proteins are degraded into amino acids, which after deamination can enter the respiratory pathway as pyruvate, acetyl CoA, or other intermediates of the Krebs cycle.

Thus, acetyl CoA is the common intermediate metabolite for the aerobic breakdown of glucose, amino acids, and fatty acids.

Answer: Acetyl Co.A

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In Yeast, the pyruvate is decarboxylated to form----
questions.solution:
In yeast, during anaerobic respiration (alcoholic fermentation), pyruvic acid undergoes incomplete oxidation in two steps.

First, pyruvic acid is decarboxylated to form acetaldehyde and carbon dioxide. This reaction is catalyzed by the enzyme pyruvate decarboxylase.

Second, acetaldehyde is reduced to ethyl alcohol (ethanol) by the enzyme alcohol dehydrogenase, utilizing NADH + H$^{+}$.

Therefore, the direct product of the decarboxylation of pyruvate is acetaldehyde.

Answer: Acetaldehyde

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Which of the following reactions is the decarboxylation reaction?
questions.solution:
In the Krebs cycle, oxalosuccinic acid is a 6-carbon compound that undergoes decarboxylation to form $\alpha$-ketoglutarate, which is a 5-carbon compound, releasing one molecule of $CO_2$.

Succinic acid $\rightarrow$ Fumaric acid is an oxidation (dehydrogenation) reaction where $FAD$ is reduced to $FADH_2$.

Cis-aconitate $\rightarrow$ Isocitric acid is a hydration reaction involving the addition of a water molecule.

Isocitrate $\rightarrow$ Oxalosuccinic acid is an oxidation (dehydrogenation) reaction where $NAD^+$ is reduced to $NADH + H^+$.

Therefore, the decarboxylation reaction is Oxalosuccinic acid $\rightarrow$ $\alpha$-Ketoglutarate.

Answer: Oxalosuccinic acid $\rightarrow$ $\alpha$-Ketoglutarate

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: During respiration, Oxidative phosphorylation occurs in
questions.solution:
Oxidative phosphorylation is the process of ATP synthesis coupled with the transfer of electrons through the electron transport chain (ETC) to oxygen.

The complexes of the electron transport chain and the enzyme ATP synthase, which are responsible for oxidative phosphorylation, are embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane.

Glycolysis occurs in the cytoplasm, while the Krebs cycle takes place in the mitochondrial matrix.

Answer: Inner Mitochondrial membrane only

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: In the ETS, electrons from $\text{FADH}_2$ enter into which complex?
questions.solution:
In the electron transport system (ETS), electrons from $\text{NADH}$ are oxidized by Complex I (NADH dehydrogenase).

Electrons from $\text{FADH}_2$ are oxidized by Complex II (succinate dehydrogenase).

Therefore, electrons from $\text{FADH}_2$ enter the ETS through Complex II.

Answer: Complex II

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Cytochrome C is oxidized by complex IV [cytochrome C oxidase] consisting of----
questions.solution:
Complex IV in the electron transport chain is also known as cytochrome c oxidase.

It is responsible for the oxidation of cytochrome c and the reduction of oxygen to water.

Complex IV consists of cytochromes a and $a_3$, along with two copper centers ($Cu_A$ and $Cu_B$).

Answer: Cytochrome a and $a_3$

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: Substrate level phosphorylation occurs --------------------.
options: in cytoplasm of the cells only.
in cytoplasm of the cells and matrix of mitochondria.
questions.solution:
Substrate level phosphorylation is the direct synthesis of ATP from ADP by the transfer of a phosphate group from a phosphorylated reactive intermediate.

During glycolysis, which takes place in the cytoplasm, substrate level phosphorylation occurs at two steps: the conversion of $1,3$-bisphosphoglycerate to $3$-phosphoglyceric acid and the conversion of phosphoenolpyruvate to pyruvic acid.

During the Krebs cycle, which takes place in the mitochondrial matrix, substrate level phosphorylation occurs during the conversion of succinyl-CoA to succinic acid, where a molecule of GTP (which is then used to form ATP) is synthesized.

Therefore, substrate level phosphorylation occurs in both the cytoplasm of the cells and the matrix of mitochondria.

Answer: in cytoplasm of the cells and matrix of mitochondria.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.q: During glycolysis, which one of the following intermediates undergoes oxidation as well as phosphorylation?
questions.solution:
